refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analysis of terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, these should be separated into distinct FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain analysis, avoiding any implementation details or combined requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an analysis of the worksite terrain independently of other features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Create a semantic map annotating terrain with safety and operational attributes
Produce a layered map separating physical terrain data from policy overlays
Develop a modular terrain analysis output that can be queried by planning and execution modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Produce a layered map separating physical terrain data from policy overlays

potential_sources: Mixing physical terrain data with policy information could create dependencies between data updates and policy changes
coupling_check.notes: By separating physical terrain data from policy overlays, the design supports independent updates and reduces coupling between terrain sensing and policy management.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that terrain data and policy information can be cleanly separated and integrated at higher layers
Risk that policy changes may require re-interpretation of terrain data if overlays are not well defined
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume layered map format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the interface and data format standards for the layered map to ensure interoperability and ease of integration with planning and execution functions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing and data management functions, including the production of layered maps that separate physical terrain data from policy overlays.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and terrain data inputs.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and policy overlay instructions.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed layered map data to other modules or systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the NGHE Solution is responsible for data processing and map production; minimal diffusion as most data processing is internal.
Low containment since Vehicle focuses on physical operation; would require significant diffusion to handle map layering.
Moderate containment as Teleoperation may use maps but does not produce them; would cause diffusion if tasked with map layering.
Very low containment; Safety Services focus on alerts and safety, not map production; high diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the DP to produce layered maps separating physical terrain data from policy overlays, as it naturally contains the data processing and map generation responsibilities with minimal diffusion outside the module.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Telemetry and Tasking inputs may require careful interface design.
If policy overlays change frequently, updates may cause diffusion into other modules if not well encapsulated.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP to produce a layered map separating physical terrain data from policy overlays directly addresses the FR to provide analysis of the worksite terrain by logically decomposing the requirement into distinct data layers, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of generating a worksite model/map including site features and policies.

diffusion_risks: Potential diffusion risk exists if policy overlays change frequently and require updates across multiple modules beyond NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal scattering of the DP across multiple IMs. The module boundary encompasses core processing and data management functions, including layered map production, which supports good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through the NGHE Solution's interfaces: Telemetry and Tasking inputs provide necessary data and commands, and the Network Link outputs the layered map to planning/execution functions, enabling validation against acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities to the NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces supports traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface complexity between NGHE Solution and Telemetry/Tasking modules to ensure clean separation and minimize integration issues.
Establish encapsulation strategies for policy overlays to prevent diffusion into other modules if policy changes are frequent.
Document update procedures for policy overlays within NGHE Solution to maintain containment and reduce diffusion risk.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and containment, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirements with clear design parameters and implementation modules. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and although some diffusion risks exist, recommended revisions are provided to monitor and mitigate them without requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable architecture is preferable to avoid complexity from deeper decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: To address potential diffusion risks and interface complexity, revisions are recommended to monitor interface complexity, establish encapsulation strategies for policy overlays, and document update procedures within the NGHE Solution module.

assumptions: The current DP and IM sufficiently satisfy the FR for the worksite terrain analysis.
The NGHE Solution module's high containment score indicates strong architectural packaging.
Verification through existing interfaces is feasible and reliable.
open_risks: Potential diffusion risk if policy overlays change frequently and require updates across multiple modules beyond NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Monitoring data on interface complexity between NGHE Solution and Telemetry/Tasking modules.
Documentation and validation of encapsulation strategies for policy overlays.
Procedures for updating policy overlays within NGHE Solution to ensure containment and reduce diffusion risk.
